articles.title: Seven Investigative Story Templates You Can Steal and Run Anywhere in the World
articles.summary: GIJN compiled a field guide of underreported story archetypes -- pension fund black holes, land seizures masked as development, femicide data gaps -- that replicate across borders with disturbing consistency. The real utility here: each template comes with sourcing strategies, FOI angles, and cross-border collaboration tips that turn a local hunch into a publishable investigation.

articles.title: The Netflix Data Says Emotional Documentaries Win by 2.3x -- Here's How to Structure One
articles.summary: A breakdown of what separates forgettable history docs from ones people actually finish: non-linear timelines, conflict-first structure borrowed from feature films, and the Ken Burns insight that "not knowing how something turns out" matters more than the facts themselves.

articles.title: Your Audience Can't See Your Nerves -- But They Can See Your Energy
articles.summary: Jon Youshaei's framework for camera confidence boils down to one counterintuitive move: reframe anxiety as excitement, because your body can't tell the difference. Practical tactics include the sticky-note-on-the-lens trick for eye contact and pre-record pump-up songs to replace missing co-host energy.

articles.title: The 2026 AI Script Stack That Lets Non-Native Speakers Write Like They Grew Up in Brooklyn
articles.summary: A ranked comparison of AI script generators -- Subscribr, Jasper, Writesonic, vidIQ -- evaluated specifically for multilingual creators who draft in English but think in another language. The move: use AI for structure and idiom, then polish voice manually.
